items.q: How do you say 'I do not eat' in French?
items.why: Students forget the verb 'sandwich'. Ne and pas wrap AROUND the verb in present tense - ne BEFORE the verb, pas AFTER. In compound tenses, they wrap around the auxiliary.

items.correct: As a nasal vowel - the n is not fully pronounced.
items.why: Students pronounce nasal vowels like English consonants. French 'on', 'an', 'in', 'un' are nasalised vowels - air resonates through the nose but the n consonant itself isn't articulated.

items.q: Which verbs take 'être' as their auxiliary in the passé composé?
items.correct: Movement/state-change verbs (DR & MRS VAN DER TRAMP) and all reflexive verbs.
items.why: Students forget reflexives. Both groups use être: aller/venir/monter, etc. AND every reflexive verb (je me suis lavé). With être, the past participle agrees with the subject.

items.q: Which auxiliary does 'se marier' take in the passé composé?
items.why: Correct answer: être. “avoir” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: blended family. “nuclear family” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year was the PACS introduced in France?
items.why: The real figure is 1999. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
